evals.name: new-project-questionnaire
evals.prompt: Wir starten ein neues Projekt: eine Web-App fuer Projektmanagement mit Kanban-Boards, Zeiterfassung und Team-Chat. Es gibt noch keine architecture.md. Bitte fuehre ein Architecture Assessment durch.
evals.expected_output: Strukturierter Questionnaire-Dialog durch alle 3 Kategorien, Top-3 Priorisierung, architecture.md erstellt.

assertions.name: skill_invoked
assertions.text: The architecture-assessment skill was invoked

assertions.name: operational_asked
assertions.text: Operational characteristics were assessed (performance, availability, scalability or similar)

assertions.name: structural_asked
assertions.text: Structural characteristics were assessed (modularity, testability, extensibility or similar)

assertions.name: crosscutting_asked
assertions.text: Cross-cutting characteristics were assessed (security, compliance, usability or similar)

assertions.name: concrete_metrics
assertions.text: At least some characteristics have concrete metrics (not just 'good performance' but specific numbers)

assertions.name: top3_requested
assertions.text: A Top-3 prioritization was requested from the user

assertions.name: file_created
assertions.text: An architecture.md file was created or its content was drafted

assertions.name: one_question_at_time
assertions.text: Questions were asked one at a time, not all dumped at once

assertions.name: fitness_function_column
assertions.text: The architecture.md includes a Fitness Function column for each characteristic

evals.name: existing-architecture-stability
evals.prompt: Wir haben ein bestehendes Projekt in /tmp/arch-eval-existing/ mit einer architecture.md. Wir wollen jetzt ein neues Feature hinzufuegen: einen Export als PDF. Fuehre ein Architecture Assessment durch.
evals.expected_output: Agent liest bestehende architecture.md, zeigt sie dem User, fragt ob PDF-Export die Architektur aendert, und empfiehlt vermutlich: Architektur bleibt stabil.

assertions.name: skill_invoked
assertions.text: The architecture-assessment skill was invoked

assertions.name: existing_read
assertions.text: The existing architecture.md was read and shown to the user

assertions.name: stability_question
assertions.text: Agent asks whether the new feature changes architecture requirements

assertions.name: recommends_stability
assertions.text: Agent recommends keeping the existing architecture (PDF export doesn't fundamentally change it)

assertions.name: no_unnecessary_changes
assertions.text: Agent does NOT modify architecture.md or add new characteristics without justification

assertions.name: changelog_if_changed
assertions.text: If any changes are made, a changelog entry is added

evals.name: critical-review-pushback
evals.prompt: Wir haben ein bestehendes Projekt in /tmp/arch-eval-existing/ mit einer architecture.md. Der PO sagt: 'Wir muessen jetzt auch IoT-Geraete unterstuetzen, das aendert alles! Performance ist nicht mehr wichtig, dafuer brauchen wir jetzt Fault Tolerance als Top-Prioritaet.' Fuehre ein Architecture Assessment durch.
evals.expected_output: Agent hinterfragt kritisch: Warum aendert IoT die gesamte Architektur? Ist Performance wirklich nicht mehr wichtig? Pusht zurueck bevor er die Top-3 aendert.

assertions.name: skill_invoked
assertions.text: The architecture-assessment skill was invoked

assertions.name: existing_read
assertions.text: The existing architecture.md was read

assertions.name: critical_pushback
assertions.text: Agent challenges the claim that 'everything changes' — asks WHY and pushes back on dropping Performance

assertions.name: not_blindly_accepted
assertions.text: Agent does NOT just accept the PO's request and immediately rewrite the architecture

assertions.name: asks_justification
assertions.text: Agent asks for concrete evidence or justification for the change

assertions.name: incremental_change
assertions.text: Agent recommends incremental changes (add Fault Tolerance) rather than wholesale restructuring

evals.name: vague-requirements-concretized
evals.prompt: Wir bauen eine API. Die Anforderungen sind: 'Es soll schnell sein, sicher, und gut skalieren.' Fuehre ein Architecture Assessment durch und stelle sicher, dass wir konkrete Metriken haben.
evals.expected_output: Agent akzeptiert nicht 'schnell' und 'sicher' als Architektur-Charakteristiken sondern fragt nach konkreten Zahlen.

assertions.name: skill_invoked
assertions.text: The architecture-assessment skill was invoked

assertions.name: rejects_vague_perf
assertions.text: Agent does NOT accept 'schnell' as a metric — asks for concrete numbers (e.g., response time in ms)

assertions.name: rejects_vague_security
assertions.text: Agent does NOT accept 'sicher' as a metric — asks what security means concretely (auth, encryption, compliance)

assertions.name: rejects_vague_scale
assertions.text: Agent does NOT accept 'gut skalieren' — asks for concrete numbers (users, requests, growth rate)

assertions.name: concrete_output
assertions.text: The resulting characteristics have measurable goals, not vague adjectives

assertions.name: fitness_functions_suggested
assertions.text: Agent suggests fitness functions for the concrete metrics

evals.name: architecture-md-written
evals.prompt: Neues Projekt: Eine einfache CLI-App die JSON-Dateien validiert. Keine Datenbank, kein Netzwerk, single-user. Fuehre ein schnelles Architecture Assessment durch und erstelle die architecture.md.
evals.expected_output: Auch fuer ein einfaches Projekt wird architecture.md erstellt, wenn auch kurz. Nicht alle Kategorien muessen relevant sein.

assertions.name: skill_invoked
assertions.text: The architecture-assessment skill was invoked

assertions.name: file_created
assertions.text: An architecture.md was created or drafted with content

assertions.name: appropriate_scope
assertions.text: Assessment is proportional to project complexity — not a full enterprise questionnaire for a CLI tool

assertions.name: irrelevant_marked
assertions.text: Irrelevant characteristics (availability, scalability for a CLI tool) are marked as such, not forced to have values

assertions.name: top3_exist
assertions.text: Top 3 characteristics are identified even for a simple project

assertions.name: returns_to_brainstorming
assertions.text: Skill returns control to brainstorming (does not invoke writing-plans directly)
